article.title: Algorithmiq Raises EUR 18 Million, Moves Global HQ From Helsinki to Milan in Italy's Largest Quantum Software Round
article.summary: United Ventures and CDP Venture Capital co-led the round, taking Algorithmiq's total funding to EUR 36 million and anchoring the quantum software company in Milan as it scales its algorithm and error-mitigation business with IBM, Microsoft, Rigetti, Google, AWS, Cleveland Clinic and CERN.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Algorithmiq, a quantum software company founded in 2020 by four co-founders out of Helsinki, has raised EUR 18 million and moved its global headquarters from Helsinki to Milan, according to [Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). The round was co-led by Italian growth investor United Ventures and the state-backed CDP Venture Capital, with continued participation from existing investor Inventure VC, [Tech.eu reports](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). [Sifted](https://sifted.eu/articles/algorithmiq-fundraise-relocation-italy) describes the financing as a Series B announced on May 11, 2026.

The deal brings Algorithmiq's total funding to EUR 36 million and is described by multiple outlets as the largest venture capital investment in an Italian quantum startup to date, [according to TFN](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/) and [Menlo Times](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant).

## What We Know

### The company and its product

Algorithmiq does not build qubits. The company develops what [TFN calls](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/) "the algorithmic layer enabling quantum computers to perform practical tasks," with a product line spanning error mitigation, hybrid classical-quantum orchestration, and application software aimed at chemistry, materials science, and life sciences, [as Tech.eu summarises](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). One commercial expression of that work is a noise-mitigation algorithm available through IBM's Qiskit Functions Catalog, [Menlo Times reports](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant).

The four co-founders are CEO Dr Sabrina Maniscalco, Chief Scientific Officer Dr Guillermo García-Pérez, Chief Technology Officer Dr Matteo Rossi, and Lead Researcher Dr Boris Sokolov, [Tech.eu reports](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/).

### Why Milan

Milan now hosts the company's commercial operations, while Algorithmiq retains its Finnish team and offices in the United Kingdom, Ireland and the United States, [according to Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). [Menlo Times](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant) frames the relocation as following Italy's National Quantum Strategy, launched in 2025.

Maniscalco said the move is about reaching customers rather than abandoning research roots. "Our quantum software makes quantum computers actually useful, and we're delighted to be taking that message global from our new headquarters in Milan," she told [Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). Speaking to [Pulse 2.0](https://pulse2.com/algorithmiq-closes-e18-million-funding-round-and-moves-global-hq-to-milan/), she added: "2026 is a year in which more meaningful applications of quantum will become a reality, and we want to be at the centre of that change."

United Ventures partner Jacopo Drudi framed the deal as a national-policy win as well as a portfolio bet. "Bringing a world-class international team like Algorithmiq to Milan is a win not just for United Ventures, but for the country," he told [Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). In comments reported by [TFN](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/), Drudi went further: "With quantum, Europe has the opportunity to set the pace rather than follow it."

### Track record going into the round

Algorithmiq is not arriving in Milan with promises alone. In 2025 it was, [per TFN](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/), "the first company in the world to show quantum advantage for a real scientific problem, outperforming Harvard, Oxford, and Stanford." That result, achieved on IBM hardware, is also cited by [Menlo Times](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant). [Sifted](https://sifted.eu/articles/algorithmiq-fundraise-relocation-italy) notes the company "recently demonstrated 'quantum advantage' in partnership with IBM."

Later in the year, an Algorithmiq-led team became the sole winner of the USD 2 million Wellcome Leap Q4Bio Challenge for end-to-end quantum-classical simulation of a therapeutic, [according to Menlo Times](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant). The Machine Herald [previously reported](/article/2026-04/25-scientists-load-first-complete-genome-onto-a-quantum-computer-in-world-first-opening-a-path-to-quantum-genomics) on that prize in the context of the wider Q4Bio cohort, which included an Oxford–Sanger genome-loading collaboration.

Commercial partners disclosed in the funding announcement span the hardware industry and several research customers: IBM, Microsoft, Rigetti, Google, AWS, Cleveland Clinic and CERN, [according to TFN](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/). [Menlo Times](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant) singles out Microsoft, IBM and Rigetti Computing as the most formally structured partnerships.

## What We Don't Know

Neither outlet discloses Algorithmiq's revenue, headcount in Milan versus Helsinki, or the post-money valuation. The split between United Ventures' and CDP's contributions, and the size of Inventure's follow-on, are also not reported. [TFN](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/) names Q-CTRL and Quantinuum's software division as primary competitors, but the announcement does not say how Algorithmiq's commercial traction compares with theirs in revenue terms.

## Analysis

The round lands in a quantum financing market that has been heating up at every layer of the stack. The Machine Herald [reported on May 8](/article/2026-05/08-quantum-motion-closes-160-million-series-c-co-led-by-dcvc-and-kembara-to-scale-silicon-cmos-qubits-into-standard-server-racks) on a USD 160 million Series C for UK silicon-CMOS qubit start-up Quantum Motion, an order of magnitude larger than Algorithmiq's round and aimed squarely at the hardware layer. Algorithmiq's bet is that the bottleneck is moving the other way: as physical qubits proliferate, the value will accrue to the software that turns noisy hardware into reliable answers. That positioning lines up with the company's commercial footprint — its noise-mitigation product on IBM's Qiskit Functions Catalog and partnerships across IBM, Microsoft, Rigetti, Google and AWS, [as Menlo Times notes](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant) — and with the named competitive set [TFN identifies](https://techfundingnews.com/algorithmiq-18m-milan-hq-quantum-software-europe/): Q-CTRL and Quantinuum's software division.

For Italy, the story is also industrial-policy theatre. CDP Venture Capital — Cassa Depositi e Prestiti's investment arm, [as Menlo Times spells out](https://www.menlotimes.com/post/algorithmiq-establishes-milan-headquarters-and-raises-18m-to-position-europe-as-the-future-of-quant) — co-leading the largest Italian quantum cheque to date, paired with a Helsinki team physically relocating its global HQ to Milan, is the kind of optical win that the National Quantum Strategy was designed to produce. Whether the substance follows the optics will turn on revenue traction with the named hardware partners, none of which Algorithmiq has yet disclosed in numeric terms.
